title: Timers: sleep, start_timer, cancel_timer, with_timeout
purpose: Give the author durable waiting: an anonymous sleep, named cancellable timers, and a with_timeout combinator that bounds an awaiting operation. All over the unit-safe Duration type, all binding to AT's two-tier timer mechanics.
task: Implement workflow/timer.gleam: sleep(Duration) (anonymous durable timer), start_timer(name, Duration) returning a cancellable reference, cancel_timer(reference) (no-op if already fired), and with_timeout wrapping an awaiting operation with a Duration deadline returning a typed TimedOut on expiry. Bind each to AT's timer mechanics via the ffi layer.

requirements.title: sleep — anonymous durable timer
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define workflow.sleep(Duration) binding to an anonymous durable timer (engine-assigned TimerId) that is not separately cancellable.
acceptance: workflow/timer.gleam defines sleep(Duration) -> Nil (or Result for the cancellation-originated case), binding to AT's anonymous-sleep mechanism via the ffi layer.
A doc comment states the sleep is durable (survives restart, replayed instantly if already fired) and that cancelling a sleep means cancelling the workflow (AT D3); it is not separately cancellable.
A harness test advances simulated time past the sleep and asserts the workflow proceeds.

requirements.title: start_timer / cancel_timer — named cancellable timers
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define workflow.start_timer(name, Duration) returning a timer reference and workflow.cancel_timer(reference) cancelling it, with cancelling an already-fired timer being a no-op.
acceptance: start_timer(name: String, Duration) returns a typed timer reference (carrying the TimerId) bound to AT's named-timer mechanism.
cancel_timer(reference) disarms the timer; cancelling an already-fired timer is a no-op (not an error), matching AT D3.
A harness test starts a timer, cancels it before advancing past it, and asserts it does not fire; a second test fires it and asserts cancel is a no-op.

requirements.title: with_timeout combinator
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define workflow.with_timeout bounding an awaiting operation with a Duration deadline, returning a typed TimedOut on expiry.
acceptance: with_timeout takes an awaiting operation (e.g. the result of workflow.receive) and a Duration, returning Result(value, TimedOut)-shaped output using the engine-originated timeout variant from AF-003.
On expiry it returns the typed timeout error; on completion before the deadline it returns the value.
A harness test asserts both paths: a receive that arrives within the deadline returns Ok, and one that does not returns the typed timeout.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the timer wheel, durable timer recording, or recovery — those are AT-002/AT-003/AT-004. This brief binds to them.
SHALL NOT define signal receive itself — with_timeout wraps it; the receive is AF-007.
SHALL NOT introduce a default timer duration.
verification: gleam test passes for sleep, named-timer, and with_timeout tests under the harness.
No panic/todo in new code; durations are Duration, not bare Int.
